playbook.intent: Approved RFC → live production behind a feature flag with telemetry, runbook, and post-launch review scheduled.
playbook.scope: One product bet travels from an approved RFC to live production. All handoffs (PM brief → BA reqs → Architect design → Dev implementation → QA verification → DevOps deploy → PdM rollout) are auditable. Shipping closes the loop with a feature flag, telemetry, an on-call runbook, and a post-launch review scheduled. The single living artifact under `.aidocs/in-progress/<feature>/` is the unit of work; tracker is the mirror, not the source.

success-criteria.criterion: spec.md + design.md + implementation-plan.md exist and are coherent
Acceptance criteria mapped 1:1 to tests (contract + property + mutation)
Feature behind a flag with dark/ramp/GA states defined
Telemetry: Grafana dashboards + Prometheus alerts + Sentry/Datadog wiring
On-call runbook published with tagged steps

stage.name: RFC → spec
stage.intent: PM brief + BA elicitation produce a spec with acceptance criteria.

tasks.task: Define acceptance criteria + Definition of Ready

stage.decision-gate: Advance when spec passes Definition of Ready: AC are testable, scope cuts are explicit, dependencies named.

stage.name: Architect → design
stage.intent: Architect commits a design.md with quality attributes, boundaries, and reuse decisions.

tasks.task: Run quality-attributes analysis (perf, sec, reliability, observability)
Decide on microservices/event-driven boundaries where applicable
Author CLAUDE.md updates so agents stay LLM-friendly

stage.decision-gate: Advance when design.md is reviewed in the weekly architectural review and CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md updates land.

stage.name: Dev → implementation
stage.intent: Plan-mode locked, worktree-isolated, red-green-split, contract-tested implementation.

tasks.task: Operate in plan-mode then locked execution

stage.decision-gate: Advance when implementation matches spec, all gates green, conventional commits enforced.

stage.name: QA → verification
stage.intent: Property-based + mutation + contract tests prove the AC. Self-healing locators audited.

tasks.task: Add property-based invariants for LLM-touched paths
Wire self-healing locators with audit log

outputs.output: Test suite with traceability to AC

stage.decision-gate: Advance when every AC traces to at least one passing test and code gates are green.

stage.name: DevOps → deploy
stage.intent: GitOps deploy with progressive rollout behind a flag; telemetry + runbook live.

tasks.task: Publish on-call runbook as markdown tagged steps

stage.decision-gate: Advance when flag is live, dashboards show green telemetry, runbook is committed and discoverable.

stage.name: PdM → rollout
stage.intent: Ramp the flag, measure adoption, schedule post-launch review.

tasks.task: Wire product analytics on the new path
Drive feedback loop via PdM's feedback-management methodology

stage.decision-gate: Advance once GA criteria are met OR a kill decision is documented. No flag stays dark forever.

extra.value: [RFC drafting / approval process — assumed already done upstream, Quarter-level prioritisation — handled in quarter-planning-okr-reset, Major migrations — see big-migration-postgres-monolith]
[Approved RFC referencing OKR + bet, Feature flag platform in place, CI/CD pipeline with progressive delivery + supply-chain scans, Repo wired with CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md context pyramid]

[Skipping design.md because spec 'feels small' — coupling accidents bite later, Tests added after merge — defeats traceability, 'Deploy without a flag — no rollback path, no ramp', Telemetry wired after launch — first incident has no dashboards, Runbook lives in someone's head — fails the bus-factor test]
['Can a new on-call open the runbook and follow it cold?', 'Does every AC have a test?', 'Is the flag killable in <5 min if telemetry goes red?', 'Is the post-launch review on someone''s calendar?']

{human: faion get-content rfc-to-production-feature-delivery --format md, agent: faion get-content rfc-to-production-feature-delivery --format context, json: faion get-content rfc-to-production-feature-delivery --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: Initial draft for F-064 P6 product-dev team persona}]
